channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Women have dominated ‘Tournament of Champions.’ Here’s what winners say is a key ingredient
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Maneet Chauhan delicately sprinkled saffron onto her gushtaba goat meatballs as the live audience began the final countdown. She and competitor Antonia Lofaso scrambled to finish their dishes on the Season 5 finale of Food Network’s “Tournament of Champions.
item.dc:creator: AKIRA OLIVIA KUMAMOTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man to plead guilty to 2023 shooting of Black teen Ralph Yarl, 2 people familiar with case tell AP
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — An 86-year-old Kansas City man will plead guilty Friday to the 2023 shooting of Ralph Yarl, a Black honor student who rang the white man’s doorbell by mistake, two people familiar with the case told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: KIA BREAUX and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Michigan judge sentences Walmart shoplifters to wash cars in the parking lot
item.description: A Michigan judge is putting sponges in the hands of shoplifters, ordering them to wash cars in a Walmart parking lot when spring arrives.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: WATCH: Driver in Utah escapes car before train smashes into vehicle
item.description: Video appears to show that the SUV may have been pushed onto the tracks by another vehicle, just as the crossing gates were beginning to lower.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday during a meeting with U.S. Vice President JD Vance that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks to end the Ukraine-Russia war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A drone pierced the outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant. Radiation levels are normal
item.description: CHERNOBYL NUCLEAR POWER STATION, Ukraine (AP) — A drone armed with a warhead hit the protective outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, punching a hole in the structure and briefly starting a fire, in an attack Kyiv blamed on Russia. The Kremlin denied it was responsible.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and EFREM LUKATSKY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Europe quietly works on a plan to send troops to Ukraine for post-war security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Increasingly alarmed that U.S. security priorities lie elsewhere, a group of European countries has been quietly working on a plan to send troops into Ukraine to help enforce any future peace settlement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Southern California hit by destructive debris flows caused by heavy rains
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Debris flows brought on by the strongest atmospheric river of the season swept a car off a southern California highway and into the ocean and left roads submerged in sludge.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Volunteers deliver gifts to widows on Valentine's Day. The project began in a florist's driveway
item.description: CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Jillian Myers knows the devastation of losing a husband and feeling alone and empty, particularly on Valentine's Day.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels claim to have seized second Congo airport in advance towards Bukavu
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels in eastern Congo claimed Friday to have seized a second airport in the region following a days-long advance, as the U.N. warned that the recent escalation of fighting with government forces has left 350,000 internally displaced people without shelter.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: American beauty queen charged in ex-NFL husband's murder plot granted release from Bahamas prison: report
item.description: Georgia mom Lindsay Shiver, who is accused of plotting to hire a hitman to kill her estranged husband, ex-NFL player Robert Shiver is reportedly being released on bail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Points Guy shares his favorite travel websites
item.description: Brian Kelly is better known as The Points Guy. He’s perfected the art of travel hacking for over 15 years and is now sharing his secrets. "It started as a fun little blog that just sort of blew up," said Kelly, who recently visited the KTLA studios.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Fox 5

item.title: Mexico threatens to sue Google over Gulf of Mexico name change
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum on Thursday threatened to sue Google over its decision to update its maps platform to reflect President Donald Trump’s decision to rename the Gulf of Mexico as the “Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pope Francis hospitalized to treat bronchitis, undergo tests as Holy Year events canceled
item.description: Pope Francis was hospitalized Friday to treat a weeklong bout of bronchitis and undergo diagnostic tests, the Vatican said, confirming the latest issues with the 88-year-old's pontiff's health that forced him to cancel his agenda through Monday at least.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Where US adults think the government is spending too much, according to AP-NORC polling
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Many U.S. adults believe the federal government is overspending — but polling also shows that many Americans, including Republicans, think the country is spending too little on major government programs such as Social Security.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Order to drop New York mayor's case rocks Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dozens of vehicles involved in Oregon highway crash amid 'whiteout' conditions
item.description: Dozens of vehicles, including cars, trucks and semi-trucks, were involved in crashes along Oregon's Interstate 84 early Thursday afternoon, according to the Multnomah County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump wants states to clean up forests to stop wildfires. But his administration cut off funds
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is holding up money for wildfire mitigation projects funded through legislation championed by his Democratic predecessor, threatening efforts to prevent catastrophic blazes like the ones that recently ripped through Southern California.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, MATTHEW BROWN and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration begins sweeping layoffs with probationary workers, warns of larger cuts to come
item.description: The Trump administration is intensifying its sweeping efforts to shrink the size of the federal workforce by ordering agencies to lay off all probationary employees who had not yet gained civil service protection.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: US hits international court's top prosecutor with sanctions after Trump's order
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.S. sanctioned the International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor Thursday, following up on President Donald Trump’s order last week targeting the court over its investigations of Israel.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alaska US senators introduce bill that would again designate North America's tallest peak as Denali
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Alaska's Republican U.S. senators have introduced legislation seeking to designate North America’s tallest peak as Denali — weeks after President Donald Trump issued an executive order calling for the name to revert to Mount McKinley. U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman sues city over skeleton Christmas decorations
item.description: Alexis Luttrell put a skeleton and a skeleton dog in her yard in October for Halloween and later dressed them up for the holidays.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor to allow immigration officials to operate at Rikers jail after Trump border czar meeting
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams says he’ll allow federal immigration officials to operate at the city’s Rikers Island jail following a meeting Thursday with President Donald Trump’s border czar. Adams said he’ll issue an executive order reestablishing U.S.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC subway rider who gave birth on train identified as missing Florida woman: report
item.description: A woman who gave birth on a New York City subway has been identified as missing woman out of Florida who was last seen by her family in August 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Igloo recalling 1M+ coolers after fingertip amputations
item.description: Igloo has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures and slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Humpback whale briefly swallows kayaker
item.description: A humpback whale briefly swallowed a kayaker off Chilean Patagonia before quickly releasing him unharmed. The incident, caught on camera, quickly went viral.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: What's an atmospheric river? A pineapple express? AP explains the weather phenomenon
item.description: Atmospheric rivers are a plume of moisture that extend across the Pacific to near Hawaii. When they hit land they can cause heavy rains and snow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NIH reportedly loses two top leaders in two days
item.description: Two key staff members at the National Institutes of Health (NIH) are reportedly leaving the agency amid ongoing efforts by the Trump administration and Department of Government Efficiency head Elon Musk to drastically cut down the size of the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's pick for education chief sketches a roadmap for dismantling the department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Linda McMahon on Thursday sketched out how key functions of the Education Department could be carved up to achieve President Donald Trump's goal of dismantling the agency, vowing to “reorient” the department while continuing some of its largest programs.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Egg prices hit record high of $4.95, will likely keep climbing
item.description: Of course, that is only the nationwide average. A carton of eggs can cost $10 or more in some places. And specialized varieties, such as organic and cage-free eggs, are even more expensive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California city passes controversial homeless camping ban
item.description: The ordinance, which allows the city to fine those who help the homeless and makes it illegal for homeless people to camp on public property, was approved in a 6-1 vote after more than two hours of public comments.
item.dc:creator: Joey Horta, Fox 5

item.title: Inflation ticks up to 3 percent annual increase in January
item.description: The consumer price index (CPI) increased by 0.5 percent in January to hit an annual increase of 3.0 percent after advancing by 2.9 percent in December.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, ABC 4
